steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage links sediment presence to yellow wavelengths (560 nm and above). The conclusion claims segments flowing into lakes carry more sediment. The decisive clue is the wavelength classification: sediment = yellow (560+ nm), non-sediment = lower wavelengths.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: D directly contrasts inflow (560+ nm, sediment-rich) and outflow (below 560 nm, less sediment) wavelengths, aligning with the defined sediment-color relationship. A, B, and C either discuss unrelated factors (chlorophyll), lake interiors (not river segments), or generalize without comparing inflow/outflow. That matches choice D — D is correct.
